title: dracaena spathulata subsp. sudwallensis
intro: Dracaena spathulata subsp. sudwallensis is a rare, evergreen shrub native to California, belonging to the Asparagaceae family. The medicinal properties of Dracaena spathulata subsp. sudwallensis lie in its roots, which contain compounds like saponins and phenolic acids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Dracaena spathulata subsp. sudwallensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to address these health concerns.
